stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (planting wheat) yields a guaranteed return of $109K. Option B (planting corn) depends on rainfall: with probability 36-59% (uncertain), it yields $187K; otherwise it yields $50K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in rainfall affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $109K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $99.3K to $130.8K. Point estimate: $114.4K. Option B is preferred at most assumptions (EV_B > EV_A when P(good) > 43.1%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 43.1%, which falls within the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
